---
title: My dynamic report
subtitle: Project 2
author: Kyia Greatbatch
output: html_fragment
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, error=FALSE)

```


```{css}
body {
  background-image: url("data:image/jpeg;base64,");
}

h1 {
  background-color:	#e4a3a8;
  color: black;
  text-align: center;
  font-size: 20px;
  font-weight: bold;
}

h2 {
  background-color:	#e4a3a8;
  color: black;
  text-align: center;
  font-size: 30px;
  font-weight: bold;
}

p {
  background-color:	#ffffff;
  color: black ;

}

```


## General car observations around UOA campus

```{r, echo = F}
library(tidyverse)

form_url <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/e/2PACX-1vRScH6cXndUAJWGZAcCcv36ncF1Um64AtnndiktPxwZNNf9D1brBry1sS13P622rD8fmR912wmaYNN9/pub?output=csv"

logged_data <- read_csv(form_url)

latest_data <- logged_data %>%
  rename(car_make = 2,
         vehicle_type = 3,
         car_colour = 4,
         num_people = 5,
         damage_car = 6)

```

```{r, echo=FALSE}

less_seen_car <- latest_data$vehicle_type %>% 
  min(na.rm = TRUE) 

total_car_obs <-latest_data$car_colour %>% 
  length()

most_people <- latest_data$num_people %>% 
  max(na.rm = TRUE) 
```
This report analyses observational data collected from the different car types and their visual characteristics. The observations were taken from around the University of Auckland campus. The data that was collected was the car make or brand, the type of car (e.g., hatchback, SUV, ute), the colour, how many people were inside each vehicle, and whether there was any visible external damage. My first graph specifically looks at the car brands around campus while my second graph identifies how many different car types had external damage.

## Car obersevation graphs
# Graph 1
```{r, echo=FALSE}
## Car's make observations while walking around campus
graph_1 <- latest_data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = (fct_infreq(car_make)))) +
  geom_bar() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Car brands around campus", subtitle = "STATS 220, 2026",
       x = "What is the most common car brand driving around UOA?")



## Relationship between a car's type and if there is external damage 
graph_2 <- latest_data %>% 
  ggplot(aes(fill = damage_car, x = vehicle_type)) +
  geom_bar() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "External damage based on the car type", subtitle = "STATS 220, 2026",
       x = "vehicle type", 
       fill = "external damage to car") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.direction = "vertical") +
  coord_flip()

```
```{r}
graph_1
```

# Graph 2
```{r}
graph_2
```

# Dynamic comment
::: dynamic-comment
For the observations of car characteristics made, the car type that was observed the less amount of times was `r less_seen_car`. 
The total number of observations of cars made was `r total_car_obs`.
The max amount of people observed in a car was `r most_people`.
:::



![](https://media1.tenor.com/m/Meo6YUeN6OkAAAAC/kachow-cars.gif)
